name: Darmstadtium
name_origin: Named after Darmstadt, Germany, the city that hosts the GSI Helmholtz Centre for Heavy Ion Research where the element was discovered.

discovery.discoverer: Gesellschaft fur Schwerionenforschung (GSI) team led by Sigurd Hofmann

occurrence.abundance_summary: Darmstadtium does not occur naturally. It is a synthetic element produced in particle accelerators via cold-fusion reactions such as 62Ni + 208Pb.
states: synthetic, produced atom-by-atom in particle accelerators

reactivity_summary: No chemistry of darmstadtium has been experimentally characterized; only nuclear properties have been studied. By analogy with its lighter group 10 homologs (nickel, palladium, platinum) it is predicted to be a noble-character transition metal. Listed configuration [Rn] 5f14 6d8 7s2 is one of several predictions; relativistic calculations also suggest a possible [Rn] 5f14 6d9 7s1 ground state.
isotopes_summary: All darmstadtium isotopes are radioactive. The longest-lived confirmed isotope is 281Ds with a half-life of around 11-12 seconds, identified mainly as a decay product in chains from heavier elements such as flerovium and copernicium.

citations.source: IUPAC Periodic Table of the Elements
